title: Fluffy Pancakes
steps: In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk buttermilk, egg, melted butter, and vanilla extract.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir gently until just combined (some lumps are fine).
Heat a non-stick griddle or skillet over medium heat and lightly grease it.
Pour about 1/4 cup of batter per pancake onto the griddle.
Cook until bubbles form on the surface and edges look set, about 2–3 minutes.
Flip and cook the other side for 1–2 minutes until golden brown.
Serve immediately with maple syrup, butter, or your favourite toppings.

title: Classic Caesar Salad
steps: Wash and thoroughly dry the romaine lettuce, then chop into bite-sized pieces.
Place the lettuce into a large salad bowl.
Drizzle Caesar dressing over the lettuce and toss well to coat evenly.
Add the grated Parmesan cheese and croutons.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the salad.
Season with black pepper and toss gently once more.
Serve immediately to keep the croutons crisp.

title: Spaghetti Bolognese
steps: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add onion and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook for 1 more minute.
Add the beef mince and cook, breaking it up, until browned (about 8 minutes).
Pour in the red wine (if using) and cook for 2 minutes until mostly evaporated.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, oregano, basil, salt, and pepper.
Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, cook spaghetti in salted boiling water according to package directions until al dente.
Drain spaghetti and serve topped with the Bolognese sauce and grated Parmesan.

title: Berry Smoothie
steps: Add the frozen berries and banana to a blender.
Add the Greek yogurt and milk.
Drizzle in the honey and vanilla extract.
Blend on high speed for 30–60 seconds until smooth and creamy.
Taste and adjust sweetness with more honey if desired.
Pour into a glass and serve immediately.

title: Scrambled Eggs
steps: Crack eggs into a bowl, add milk, salt, and pepper, then whisk until well combined.
Heat butter in a non-stick pan over low-medium heat until melted and foamy.
Pour in the egg mixture.
Using a spatula, gently push the eggs from the edges toward the centre as they start to set.
Continue cooking slowly, folding gently, until eggs are just softly set and still slightly glossy.
Remove from heat (carry-over heat will finish cooking them).
Sprinkle with chives if using and serve immediately on toast.
